trivia_bullets.text: Memorial to War is the red member of the five-card Memorial cycle from Dominaria (April 27, 2018). Dominaria is defined by millennia of catastrophic war — the Brothers' War, the Ice Age, the Phyrexian Invasion — and the art's volcanic, ash-choked setting evokes regions like Shiv and Urborg, both historically devastated planes of Dominaria's surface.
sources: WebSearch: Dominaria lore, Shiv / Urborg context

trivia_bullets.text: Sacrificing for {4}{R} to destroy target land makes Memorial to War the only land-destruction land in the Memorial cycle and one of the few utility lands capable of permanently removing an opponent's land. EDHREC rank 13432 — the lowest of the five Memorials — reflecting both the high activation cost and the format-level stigma against land destruction in casual Commander.

Community sentiment on MTGSalvation: 'still a little underwhelming, but if Encroaching Wastes saw play, this will too' — identifying a niche for the card while acknowledging its inefficiency relative to Field of Ruin and similar effects.

Illustrated by Richard Wright — the only artist among the BBL-held Memorials to paint just one card in the cycle. All other Memorials are by either James Paick (Genius and Glory) or Sung Choi (Folly).

related_cards.name: Memorial to Unity
related_cards.note: green cycle member; not in BBL corpus

related_cards.note: functional comparison cited in community discourse

related_cards.name: Field of Ruin
related_cards.note: most-played contemporary land-destruction land; direct competitive context for Memorial to War's poor reception
